message:
beads: record mesh-compile triage findings from 2026-04-20 review round

Adds three new beads surfaced during the cross-agent review round on
2026-04-20 while fcp-mesh was mid-migration:

- `flywheel_connectors-bk3m6` (closed): fcp-mesh/admission const fn was
  calling `u64::min` (Ord::min), which is not yet const-stable on the
  current nightly. Fix was to branch on `<` inline. Closed at
  02:52Z after the inline fix landed.

- `flywheel_connectors-g27rl` (open → now actionable by the accompanying
  node.rs commit): documents the symptoms seen when the struct-field /
  setter / use-site additions landed without the corresponding
  `local_zones` field on MeshNode and `zones` field on PeerState.
  Kept open rather than closed so future audits can cross-link the
  before/after; the node.rs commit in this batch is the fix.

- `flywheel_connectors-kzrfp` (in_progress → now actionable by the
  accompanying gossip.rs commit): documents why the prior
  change-tracking placeholder was not IBLT-shaped and records the
  ciborium dev-only manifest mistake a concurrent agent made mid-
  migration. Kept in_progress so the downstream migration items
  (fuzz target rename, baseline doc update, `IbltPlaceholder` →
  `IbltSketch` type rename) still have a parent bead.

These are purely `.beads/issues.jsonl` additions from the daemon log
— no code moves here. Committing separately so code-change bisects
do not pull in JSONL churn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bijm.2","title":"fcp.docusign: Provisioning Automation (OAuth/JWT, base-uri discovery, webhooks)","description":"# fcp.docusign: Provisioning Automation (OAuth/JWT, base-uri discovery, webhooks)

## Goal
Automate DocuSign onboarding with minimal human steps while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- Support auth via `CredentialId`:
  - OAuth2 Authorization Code (user consent)
  - JWT Grant (service integration) if policy allows
- Base URI discovery (per-account) as required by DocuSign.
- Connect webhook setup automation:
  - register events for envelope lifecycle
  - validate webhook secret/signature configuration
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Fully mockable for unit tests.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bijm.3","title":"TEST: DocuSign Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: DocuSign Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)

## Goal
Mock-only tests for fcp.docusign envelope workflows and webhook ingestion.

## Requirements
- No real DocuSign calls in CI.
- Mock endpoints for:
  - envelope create/update/send/void
  - recipient + tab management
  - template operations
  - document download
  - Connect webhook ingestion
- Tests for:
  - document IO streaming + size limits
  - capability gating for send/void/delete
  - approval gating for dangerous ops
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic CI suite.
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bijm.4","title":"E2E: DocuSign Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: DocuSign Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Run the shared Mechanical Connector Compliance Runner against fcp.docusign.

## Must verify
- Default deny via CapabilityTokens.
- NetworkConstraints enforcement.
- Receipts/audit for envelope send/void and other dangerous ops.
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner output bundle is green for fcp.docusign.

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-bk3m6","title":"fcp-mesh/admission: const fn uses non-const Ord::min — lib test build broken","description":"**File**: crates/fcp-mesh/src/admission.rs:572-575

**Code**:
```rust
const fn prev_window_weight(&self, now_ms: u64) -> u64 {
    let elapsed = now_ms.saturating_sub(self.window_start_ms);
    WINDOW_MS.saturating_sub(elapsed.min(WINDOW_MS))
}
```

**Root cause**: `u64::min` calls the `Ord::min` trait method. `Ord` is not yet stable as a `const trait` on the current nightly (see rust-lang issue #143874). Without `#![feature(const_cmp)]` on the crate, `const fn` callers cannot invoke it.

**Compiler error**:
```
error[E0015]: cannot call non-const fn `<u64 as std::cmp::Ord>::min` in constant functions
 --> crates/fcp-mesh/src/admission.rs:574:42
error: `Ord` is not yet stable as a const trait
```

This breaks `cargo test -p fcp-mesh` lib-test compilation entirely. Callers: `effective_bytes_in_window`, `effective_symbols_in_window`, and any other `const fn` downstream.

**Fix plan (minimal)**: Replace `elapsed.min(WINDOW_MS)` with a const-compatible branch:
```rust
let clamped = if elapsed < WINDOW_MS { elapsed } else { WINDOW_MS };
WINDOW_MS.saturating_sub(clamped)
```
No behavior change. Preserves the `const fn` attribute for downstream usage. Add a smoke test that evaluates prev_window_weight in a const context (e.g., `const _: u64 = ...`) to pin the const-ness going forward.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:42:46.340215Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:52:24.137059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:52:24.136348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bngd","title":"TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests (Deterministic)


## Goal
Validate `fcp.discord` integration behavior (gateway + REST) under deterministic mocks, including event tainting and approval-gated writes.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Purpose
Integration tests for `fcp.discord` using:
- a deterministic mock Discord REST API (wiremock or in-process)
- a deterministic mock Discord Gateway (WebSocket server)

No real Discord calls are allowed.

## What this suite must prove
### 1) Gateway correctness
- IDENTIFY / RESUME handling
- heartbeat timing + ack handling
- sequence number tracking and persistence
- reconnect/backoff behavior is bounded

### 2) REST correctness
- request formation for send_message / reactions / channel list
- error mapping (401/403/429/5xx → FCP error taxonomy)
- Retry-After compliance

### 3) Singleton-writer state
- only the lease-holder maintains the gateway connection
- restart resumes safely (or fails closed if resume is unsafe)

### 4) FCP2 connector contract
- standard method surface end-to-end
- capability gating (deny without token, allow with token)
- events emitted as EventEnvelopes with correct topics
- inbound events tainted as external input

## Required adversity cases
- global rate limit
- bucketed rate limit
- malformed gateway frames
- mid-stream disconnect during event emission

## Logging requirements
- Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs.
- Never log bot tokens or raw message bodies by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failures are actionable (DecisionReceipt reason_code + evidence surfaced).

Also fixed PUT method bug in request_no_response() (was falling through to GET). fmt/clippy/all tests green.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:24:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t","title":"[Mesh] Upgrade gossip protocol from placeholder Bloom filter to real XOR filter + IBLT reconciliation","description":"The gossip protocol in crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs has real protocol logic for anti-entropy and signed summaries, but the IBLT (Invertible Bloom Lookup Table) and XOR filter implementations are acknowledged placeholders. Comments at lines 55-61 explicitly state: 'This baseline uses a simple Bloom filter approximation. Production implementations SHOULD upgrade to actual XOR filters' and 'Real XOR filter would use fingerprints + 3-way XOR'. IBLT is critical for efficient set reconciliation between mesh nodes - without it, gossip requires sending full object lists instead of compact difference summaries. The README promises 'XOR filters + IBLT for efficient gossip reconciliation' and bounds gossip to MAX_OBJECT_IDS_PER_REQUEST=100.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:51:32.495907Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T06:07:09.581665Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T06:07:09.581406Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gossip","mesh"],"comments":[{"id":2571,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: All 6 children closed. XOR filter: replaced Bloom placeholder with xorf::Xor8 (≈1.23 bits/element, <0.4% FP). IBLT: production iblt.rs module with 3-hash BLAKE3 cells, insert/delete/subtract/peel. Wired into gossip.rs with build_iblt() + reconcile_zone_iblt(). 133 gossip tests + 26 IBLT tests pass. Criterion benchmarks added.","created_at":"2026-04-07T06:07:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t.1","title":"[Mesh] Implement production XOR filter for compact set membership in gossip","description":"Replace the Bloom filter approximation in crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs with a real XOR filter (Xor8 or Xor16). XOR filters are more space-efficient than Bloom filters (use 1.23 bits/key vs ~10 bits/key) and have no false negatives. Implementation: 1) Use the xorf crate or implement Xor8 from Fan et al. 2014, 2) Build filter from set of object IDs on a node, 3) Transmit compact filter in gossip summaries, 4) Recipient queries filter to determine which objects the sender has, 5) Only request objects not in the filter. Key: fingerprint function must be deterministic (use BLAKE3 truncated to 8/16 bits). The filter should be rebuiltable from the local symbol store.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:51:33.043192Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:29:16.159464Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:29:16.159060Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gossip","mesh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:10.576423Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:51:33.043192Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2565,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-br21t.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Replaced Bloom filter approximation with real xorf::Xor8 XOR filter. Changes: (1) Added xorf 0.12 with serde feature to workspace deps and fcp-mesh/Cargo.toml. (2) Rewrote XorFilterPlaceholder to use BTreeSet<u64> keys with lazy Xor8 construction. (3) Same public API (insert, may_contain, digest, len, is_empty). (4) Digest uses FCP2-XOR-FILTER-DIGEST-V2 domain separator. (5) All 115 gossip tests pass. (6) No clippy warnings in fcp-mesh.","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:29:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.

Full saved items lifecycle. Reddit at 22 ops, tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MossyShore","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:35.521843538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629752Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1094,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"MossyShore","text":"Implemented Airtable attachment download hardening. Changes: enforce HTTPS/host/port validation against the manifest allowlist, walk redirects manually with per-hop validation, cap response size at 100 MiB before/while reading, and stop logging token-bearing attachment URLs via tracing/error messages. Added focused tests for disallowed hosts, oversized downloads, local redirect handling, and FCP error mapping. Validation completed: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-check cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-test cargo test -p fcp-airtable --test integration -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-clippy cargo clippy -p fcp-airtable --all-targets -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide cargo check was attempted via rch but fell back locally and is still blocked/contended by concurrent cargo activity from other agents, so I am leaving the bead in_progress until that clears or someone explicitly accepts the package-scoped verification.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:50:36Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented attachments.rs module: Attachment struct, AttachmentConfig (size/type limits), AttachmentValidation (URL/filename/MIME validation). 110 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","title":"fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide fast, typed Spotify search for discovery workflows.

## Scope
- Search across types:
  - track
  - album
  - artist
  - playlist
  - show/podcast
  - episode
- Support filters:
  - market/locale
  - pagination

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.search.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Spotify Web API host.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.search`
  - `spotify.search.tracks`
  - `spotify.search.albums`
  - `spotify.search.artists`
  - `spotify.search.shows`
  - `spotify.search.episodes`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Search results are stable and correctly typed.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:12.249291793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:45:44.348176Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:33.963581Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1350,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented 5 typed search operations: search.tracks, search.albums, search.artists, search.shows, search.episodes. All with optional market parameter. 113 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:33Z"},{"id":1358,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented search.rs: SearchQuery builder, SearchPage<T> generic pagination, typed results for Track/Album/Artist/Playlist/Show/Episode, SearchValidator. 151 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:45:44Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-g27rl","title":"fcp-mesh/node: in-progress local_zones wiring missing struct field — fcp-mesh lib won't compile","description":"**Files**:
- crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:315 (MeshNode struct definition)
- crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:381 (constructor init)
- crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:713-720 (update_local_zones / local_zones methods)
- crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:810 (build_planner_input use site)

**Root cause**: A concurrent agent began addressing bead **flywheel_connectors-fqzmp** (planner zones never populated) by:
- Adding `update_local_zones(&mut self, zones: HashSet<ZoneId>)` and `local_zones(&self) -> &HashSet<ZoneId>` methods
- Adding `local_zones: HashSet::new()` to the struct constructor at line 381
- Using `self.local_zones.iter()` at line 810 in build_planner_input
- Using `state.zones` for peer state in the same function

But they never added the `local_zones: HashSet<ZoneId>` field to the `MeshNode` struct definition (line 315) and never added a `zones: HashSet<ZoneId>` field to `PeerState`.

**Compiler errors** (against current main):
```
error[E0560]: struct `node::MeshNode` has no field named `local_zones`
error[E0615]: attempted to take value of method `local_zones` on type `&node::MeshNode` (×3)
error[E0308]: mismatched types
```

The fcp-mesh lib does not compile. `cargo test -p fcp-mesh` is fully blocked.

**Fix plan (urgent — restores test verification)**:
1. Add `local_zones: HashSet<ZoneId>` to the `MeshNode` struct at line 315.
2. Add `zones: HashSet<ZoneId>` (or `Vec<ZoneId>`, depending on how it's populated) to `PeerState` (struct definition needs locating).
3. Wire peer zones from MeshIdentity attestation tags via ZoneTagMapping::tag_to_zone in the handshake / gossip path.
4. Once the lib compiles, re-run the related node.rs tests at lines ~2806+ that exercise update_local_zones and confirm zone-affinity placement actually filters now.
5. Then close the parent bead **flywheel_connectors-fqzmp**.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:51:08.068785Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:51:08.068785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","title":"fcp.airtable: Full CRUD operations on records","description":"# fcp.airtable: Full CRUD Operations on Records

## Goal
Implement record CRUD with batching, pagination, and strict safety controls.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.records.list` (pagination, filter/sort)
- `airtable.records.get`
- `airtable.records.create` / `airtable.records.create_batch`
- `airtable.records.update` / `airtable.records.update_batch`
- `airtable.records.upsert`
- `airtable.records.delete` / `airtable.records.delete_batch` (Dangerous)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write vs delete are separate capabilities
- Rate limiting and backpressure are mechanical.
- Pagination:
  - support continuation tokens
  - bound page sizes
- Batch operations:
  - handle partial failures deterministically
  - stable error mapping per record
- Idempotency / retries:
  - upsert is preferred for retry-safe workflows
  - deletes and schema writes must be treated as Dangerous (policy + receipts)

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination edge cases
  - batch partial failure semantics
  - upsert conflict handling
  - redaction: never log record bodies by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- CRUD operations behave deterministically under retries.
- Dangerous operations are properly gated and audited.
","notes":"In-progress implementation slice completed: added Airtable batch update, upsert, and batch delete surfaces across client/connector/manifest/tests. Package-level verification passed via rch for fmt/check/test/clippy.

Current state after this pass:
- new operations: airtable.update_records, airtable.upsert_records, airtable.delete_records
- new client helpers and response types added
- connector validation now enforces batch size and merge-field requirements for the new paths
- integration coverage added for client + connector invoke flows

Still pending for full bead closure:
- assess whether single-record CRUD should also resolve table refs through schema for stricter consistency
- consider deterministic partial-failure mapping / richer batch error surfaces if Airtable returns mixed mutation outcomes
- complete workspace-wide verification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:33.751522695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494549Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1054,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Implemented Airtable batch update/upsert/delete plus tightened single-record and batch-create connector paths to resolve table refs through cached schema and validate fields payload shape. Added connector coverage for table-name resolution on create/create_batch/delete; package fmt/check/test/clippy are passing.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:23:28Z"},{"id":1055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Status after verification: keeping this bead IN_PROGRESS rather than closing it yet. Package-level fmt/check/test/clippy are green; workspace clippy remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing failures in connectors/box and connectors/asana.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:27:16Z"},{"id":1305,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented records.rs module: Record CRUD with builder pattern ListRecordsParams, batch operations (max 10), pagination (max 100), RecordValidator. 145 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md. Defines 7 object types: OperationReceipt (immutable execution proof), Checkpoint (state snapshot with lineage), Lease (exclusive ownership with fencing), Revocation (capability/session invalidation), ConnectorIdentity (deployment trust record), ExplanationBundle (human-readable decision justification), ConnectorState (durable runtime state). Common FcpsDurableObject header with schema versioning, content-addressing, and signatures. Includes fixtures, evolution rules, and inspection CLI commands.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-kzabz.5","title":"[Connectors] Verify supervisor recovery when connector crashes mid-invocation","description":"Prove the supervisor handles connector crashes gracefully: 1) Register a connector with fcp-host, 2) Send it a malformed JSON-RPC message that causes a panic/crash, 3) Verify supervisor detects the crash and logs it, 4) Verify supervisor restarts the connector according to its restart policy, 5) Send a valid invoke and verify the restarted connector handles it correctly, 6) Verify the audit chain records the crash event, 7) Test with RestartPolicy::OnCrash (should restart) and RestartPolicy::Never (should stay stopped). This proves the supervisor's crash recovery path, which is critical for production reliability. The supervisor code in crates/fcp-host/src/supervisor.rs has restart policies (Always, OnFailure, OnCrash, Never) and exponential backoff with jitter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:41:45.598974Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:28:09.027921Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:28:07.158674Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-kzabz.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:41:46.273230Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-kzabz.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kzabz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:41:45.598974Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2564,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-kzabz.5","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: Supervisor crash recovery tested by 190 unit tests in supervisor.rs covering: restart policies (Always/OnFailure/OnCrash/Never), crash vs clean exit behavior, exponential backoff with jitter, state machine transitions, process utilization tracking. +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-kzrfp","title":"fcp-mesh/gossip: production gossip path still uses IbltPlaceholder, not real Iblt","description":"**File**: crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs:226 (struct), 386 (use site), 1393 (decode_with_limits in handle_summary path)

**Code** (gossip.rs:226-243):
```rust
/// IBLT state placeholder for precise set reconciliation (NORMATIVE).
///
/// This baseline uses a simple change-tracking approach. Production
/// implementations SHOULD upgrade to actual IBLT for:
/// - O(d) decoding where d is the difference size
/// - Deterministic reconciliation
/// - Bounded communication overhead
pub struct IbltPlaceholder {
    recent_changes: VecDeque<(ObjectId, Option<u32>)>,
    max_changes: usize,
    change_seq: u64,
}
```

**Root cause**: An owned-and-tested production IBLT (`crates/fcp-mesh/src/iblt.rs` — `Iblt` with peeling decode, hash-check cells, IbltDecodeResult, etc.) exists and has full test coverage. But the gossip layer (`GossipState.iblt_state: IbltPlaceholder` at gossip.rs:386, plus the wire-decode path `IbltPlaceholder::decode_with_limits` at gossip.rs:1393) still uses the change-tracking placeholder. The placeholder doesn't satisfy the NORMATIVE doc claim of "precise set reconciliation" — it's just a bounded VecDeque of recent changes serialized as JSON.

**Impact**:
- Anti-entropy reconciliation between peers exchanges JSON arrays of (ObjectId, esi) recent-change tuples instead of an IBLT sketch. With more than `max_changes` divergence, the sketch silently drops the oldest changes — peers stop converging on the long tail.
- The wire format is not interoperable with what FCP3 spec describes for IBLT-based reconciliation, and the encoding is JSON rather than canonical CBOR (gossip.rs:320 `serde_json::to_vec(&self.recent_changes)`).
- Communication overhead is O(N) recent changes rather than O(d) difference, defeating the entire reason to use IBLTs.

**Fix plan**:
1. Replace `GossipState.iblt_state: IbltPlaceholder` with `Iblt` (or a wrapper that owns the per-zone Iblt + change-seq tracking).
2. Replace `IbltPlaceholder::decode_with_limits` callsite at gossip.rs:1393 with `Iblt` deserialization, bounded by cell count rather than recent-change count.
3. Wire `Iblt::insert` / `Iblt::delete` calls into the existing change-tracking hooks (`note_local_change`, etc.).
4. Reuse `Iblt::reconcile_with_peer_iblt` (already on `GossipState` at gossip.rs:563) for the actual decode.
5. Migrate any remaining `IbltPlaceholder` references in `fuzz/fuzz_targets/mesh_post_verify_summary.rs` and `docs/FCP3_Pre_Cutover_Baseline.md`.
6. Remove the placeholder struct entirely once the migration lands.
7. Add a peer-reconciliation test that exercises a difference larger than the old max_changes window — the new path should still converge; the old placeholder would have dropped changes.

**Scope warning**: This is a real architectural migration, not a 1-line fix. May need to be broken into 3-4 child beads (struct swap, wire-format change, change-tracking integration, fuzz target update).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:40:15.972415Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:51:55.777033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2796,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-kzrfp","author":"jemanuel","text":"**Update from review-mode round (2026-04-20)**: A concurrent agent has begun this migration in src/gossip.rs (already using `Iblt` and `ciborium::into_writer` at gossip.rs:365 / `ciborium::from_reader` at gossip.rs:400), but the work is broken: `ciborium` is declared in `[dev-dependencies]` only (Cargo.toml:40), not in `[dependencies]`. fcp-mesh src does not compile.

**Quick unblock**: move `ciborium.workspace = true` from `[dev-dependencies]` to `[dependencies]` in crates/fcp-mesh/Cargo.toml. The in-progress agent likely just forgot the manifest update.

Verified compile errors:
```
error[E0433]: cannot find module or crate `ciborium` in this scope
 --> crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs:365:9
 --> crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs:400:26
```","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:51:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l0v9m","title":"[Docs] Align README claims with proven reality: mark unproven features, add honest status indicators","description":"The README makes claims that are currently unproven: multi-node mesh operation, computation migration, secretless connectors (via egress proxy), intent compilation, threshold owner key. The project's own truth model says 'placeholder runtime data, guessed simulate support, and local file-edit side channels are bugs, not convenience features.' The same standard should apply to documentation. This initiative aligns the README with reality by: 1) Marking features with status indicators (Implemented/Proven/Unproven/Planned), 2) Distinguishing 'designed and coded' from 'proven end-to-end', 3) Removing claims that are currently vaporware (intent compiler). The README should be truthful about the current state while preserving the architectural vision as the target.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l0v9m.1","title":"[Docs] Add feature status indicators to README: Proven/Implemented/Designed/Planned per feature","description":"Update the 'Why Use FCP' table and all feature descriptions with honest status indicators: PROVEN (end-to-end tested with artifacts), IMPLEMENTED (code exists, unit tested), DESIGNED (types and architecture exist), PLANNED (in spec only). Current mapping: PROVEN: zone model (type system enforced), capability tokens (crypto verified), audit chain (hash linking implemented), connector lifecycle (JSON-RPC loop). IMPLEMENTED: supervisor, sandbox, egress proxy, symbol store, gossip protocol. DESIGNED: mesh multi-node, computation migration, FROST threshold key. PLANNED: intent compiler NLP, registry server.
